message:
feat(doctor): mapping atlas v3 with process spawn fallback and planner context

Mapping Atlas v3:
- EffectKind::Process now maps to ProcessSubscription (or Cmd::task
  fallback) instead of requiring custom ExtendFtui implementation
- Risk downgraded from High to Medium, policy changed from ExtendFtui
  to Approximate since the Cmd::task fallback path is well-understood
- Remediation is now automatable at Medium effort
- Add ATLAS_COMPAT_NOTES documenting upgrade path from v1 and v2

Translation Planner:
- Add PlannerContext struct that inspects IR capabilities to determine
  if ProcessSpawn is available (required or optional)
- When ProcessSpawn capability is missing, planner automatically selects
  bounded in-process Cmd::task fallback strategy instead of the native
  ProcessSubscription path
- Fallback path produces Approximate confidence with explicit rationale
  documenting the precondition mismatch
- Enumerate optional capabilities (not just required) as plan segments
  to avoid silently skipping them
- Deduplicate capabilities that appear in both required and optional sets

Doctor Tests:
- Add retry loop (up to 5 attempts with 50ms delay) around run_help_check
  calls to handle transient "Text file busy" (ETXTBSY) errors that occur
  when the OS hasn't finished closing the script file handle

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
